title: Empire of the Hand
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Empire|more=Imperial remnants|more2=Hand}} {{MultipleIssues|citation|updatecontent=[[Crisis of Faith]], [[Choices of One]]}} {{Government |image= |name=Empire of the Hand |type=[[Confederacy (disambiguation)|Confederacy]] |founding= |constitution= |headofstate=[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]]''[[Vision of the Future]]'' |headofgov=Grand Admiral |commander= |executive=A [[Unidentified council|council]] |legislative= |judicial= |military=[[Imperial Military (Empire of the Hand)|Imperial Military]]''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' |sector= |system= |celestialbody= |capital=[[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]] |language= *[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] *[[Cheunh/Legends|Cheunh]] |currency= |religious= |holiday= |anthem= |formed=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |established=Between [[19 BBY/Legends|19]] and [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]''''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|The Empire that Senior Captain Thrawn is carving into the evil that pervades our worlds is not the Empire you chose to leave. His is an Empire of justice and dignity for all beings. His Empire is one we gladly serve. One we are willing to die for.|[[Vaantaar]] describing the Empire of the Hand in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]|''[[Choices of One]]''}} The '''Empire of the Hand''', also referred to as the '''Hand''' or just the '''Empire''', was a [[Politics/Legends|political]] power located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and had its [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]]. Secretly founded by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], the Empire of the Hand was established in order to protect the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] from the many threats residing there. The Empire of the Hand functioned more like a [[Confederacy (disambiguation)|confederation]] than a true empire, forming alliances with native governments instead of conquering them. Under [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began efforts to explore and pacify the Unknown Regions, with the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] providing [[Starship/Legends|ships]] and resources to the expeditionary forces. In these yet unexplored territories, Thrawn commandeered efforts to map great swathes of territory as well as overseeing colonization and developing connections with the various species. Thrawn, seeing the varied threats in the Unknown Regions, established a new hidden military and political force to provide order and security, laying the groundwork for the Empire of the Hand. After the Galactic Empire [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|spiralled into chaos]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the Empire of the Hand emerged powerful enough to open preventive strikes on the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium]] and was also able to finally defeat and kill the warlord [[Nuso Esva]] and [[Nuso Esva's realm|his forces]] at the [[Attack on the Red City|battle]] of [[Quethold]]. With the threats in the Unknown Region seemingly quiet, the Grand Admiral decided the time had come to [[Thrawn's confederation|return]] to the known [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Under the leadership of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]], a select group of top Imperial officers, including [[General/Legends|General]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] and Commander [[Kres'ten'tarthi|Stent]], and a cadre of Thrawn-loyal Chiss upheld Thrawn's vision and believed he would return to them ten years after his announced death. The Empire of the Hand were informed by the [[New Jedi Order]] that Thrawn was indeed dead and allowed the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of Thrawn to be destroyed, with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] agreeing to keep its existence a secret. However with the clone gone, the Empire of the Hand lost its designated heir and consequently its legitimacy. However, it [[Battle of the Redoubt|assisted]] the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] against a third [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Third Vagaari War|war]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], General Soontir Fel appeared to be the leader of the Empire of the Hand and sent [[Force Spike]], a division of [[Syndic Mitth'raw'nuruodo's Household Phalanx]], to help the [[Imperial Remnant]] and New Republic. But the Empire of Hand vanished sometime during the late stages of the war, having completed its purpose as the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] began to lose to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Federation]]. However, the Empire of the Hand had ultimately survived, far from the notice of the galaxy at large. Although rumours persisted that the Empire of the Hand had either collapsed or been absorbed by another power, [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] [[Jagged Fel]] of the Imperial Remnant discovered that the Hand was still active but kept he the secret of its survival to himself for security purposes. ==History== ===Origins=== {{Quote|A work in progress, ''[…]'' But we will succeed. ''[…]'' I've seen the future, Jorj. We will succeed, because we have no other choice.|Thrawn, to Jorj Car'das|}} [[File:Trawn_and_Sidious.jpg|250px|thumb|Mitth'raw'nuruodo confers with Darth Sidious.]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] made his first contact with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]—the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] persona of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]—in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], at the time of the [[Battle for Outbound Flight|destruction]] of the ''[[Outbound Flight]]''. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and the Chiss [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] agreed with the idea that an [[Yuuzhan Vong|unknown and powerful threat]] was rising in the Unknown Regions.[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] Soon after the [[Declaration of a New Order]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]] of the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Strikefast/Legends|''Strikefast'']] discovered Thrawn in [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] on a remote [[Unidentified planet (Thrawn's exile)/Legends|planetoid]] in the Unknown Regions. Thrawn insisted he return to his people to protect them, but was convinced by Parck that by offering his service to the Galactic Empire, he would be better equipped to do so. Thrawn was convinced by Parck's reasoning and accompanied him to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].{{JournalCite|7|Mist Encounter}} Upon reaching the Imperial Capital, Thrawn formally met [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine in person. Impressed by the Chiss, Palpatine decided to have Thrawn trained as an [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]]. After rising through the ranks of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] and sharing many ideas with the Emperor about the Unknown Regions, Thrawn was tasked with a mission to explore and pacify those as yet unexplored territories. However, in order to keep the true purpose of the mission secret, and because there was still resentment among the Imperial hierarchy about the heights to which the [[Humanocentrism|non-human]] Thrawn had risen, his assignment was disguised as an undermanned cartography mission, a seeming exile as punishment for his involvement with the losing side in one of the [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial Court's]] petty feuds. In truth, the Emperor provided Thrawn with an enormous supply of ships and manpower. ===Early foundations=== {{Quote|The Unknown Regions are calling, and we have a great deal of work yet to do.|Thrawn, to Dagon Niriz|"[[Side Trip]]"}} Using the vast resources of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], Thrawn mapped great swaths of territory in the name of the Galactic Empire. [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]] and [[Dagon Niriz]], the commanders of his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]]'', were assigned to serve under Thrawn during his mission of exploration and conquest. Both men eventually became Thrawn's greatest supporters and were the first Imperial officers to join the Empire of the Hand. During his expedition to the Unknown Regions, he established laboratories, military installations, intel centres, listening posts, garrison bases, and [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] and had plotted the equivalent of 250 [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] for [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine. [[File:Admonitor-EaW.jpg|thumb|220x220px|The [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]], ''Thrawn's flagship.]] Thrawn also reestablished contact with the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]]. Many of Thrawn's own people flocked to his banner because they had been battling many dark threats from the Unknown Regions, and looked to Thrawn as a new source of hope for the Chiss. However, the Empire of the Hand was viewed with distrust by the [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]] of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], who still considered Thrawn an outcast from Chiss society, especially after his infamous [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]]. But agreeing with Thrawn's vision, many Chiss [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocras]] unofficially supported Thrawn's renegade actions.''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' In [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]], Thrawn built a vast information complex on Nirauan, the [[Hand of Thrawn]], and made it his base of operations. In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], Thrawn defeated a group of [[Ebruchi]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and captured their leader [[Creysis]]. Though he went through great lengths to avoid capture, Creysis ultimately underestimated Thrawn. Creysis was captured when a tracking device led the Chiss Admiral to his base, where Imperial forces easily overwhelmed the pirates and apprehended its leader.{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision}} ===Rise of the Hand=== {{Quote|So Thrawn went into private military training and eventually rose to the highest rank the Empire could offer him. And then, what, arranged to have himself sent back here so he could make the Chiss ruling families pay for what they'd done to him?"''"
"''Certainly not. The Chiss are his people, Mara. He has no interest in hurting them. Quite the contrary. He came back here to protect them.|[[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] and [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]]|}} [[File:Aurek_7_FB.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[501st Legion (Empire of the Hand)|501st]] [[Unit Aurek-Seven]] on [[Kariek]].]] [[4 ABY/Legends|Two years later]], Thrawn, who had been mapping the Unknown Regions, heard that Emperor Palpatine had been killed during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The Grand Admiral then returned to his Hand of Thrawn base on Nirauan. However, as the Galactic Empire collapsed, he found his forces in the Unknown Regions dwindling as ships were recalled.''[[Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts]]'' Meanwhile, the [[Ssi-ruu]]k launched a series of [[Ssi-ruuvi invasions|incursions]] into the [[The galaxy/Legends|known galaxy]], as well as near the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. Thrawn responded with preventive strikes on the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium]]. Nevertheless, the Ssi-ruuk attacked several minor Chiss worlds, capturing several Chiss as [[entechment]] subjects. Ultimately, their [[Ssi-ruuk-Chiss conflict|aggressive actions]] incurred the wrath of the [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Force]], which retaliated with a devastating [[Assault on Lwhekk|counter-attack]] on Ssi-ruuvi space. As a result of the Chiss' military strikes against the aggressors, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was able to [[Campaign in the Ssi-ruuk Star Cluster|defeat]] the Ssi-ruuk more easily.''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' By [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Imperial territory in Wild Space had become the Empire of the Hand, and finally defeated and killed the warlord Nuso Esva at the [[Attack on the Red City]].{{StoryCite|story=Crisis of Faith|book=Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition}} [[File:ThrawnDeath-TLC6.jpg|thumb|left|175px|The assassination of Thrawn]] In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Grand Admiral Thrawn returned to the known galaxy and launched a massive and brilliant [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. But despite his genius, Thrawn was betrayed and [[Death/Legends|killed]] at the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]].''[[The Last Command]]'' Thrawn had left the Empire of the Hand under the leadership of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]]. Parck, a select group of top officers, including [[General/Legends|General]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] and Commander [[Kres'ten'tarthi|Stent]], and a cadre of Thrawn-loyal Chiss were given the responsibility of upholding Thrawn's vision and expanding his empire. None of them believed that the Grand Admiral was truly dead, having been told it was another of Thrawn's strategies, as he said he would return to them ten years after his announced death. ===Post-Thrawn=== {{Quote|Many years ago Thrawn told us that if he was ever reported dead we should keep at our labors here and in the Unknown Regions, and to look for his return ten years afterward.|[[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]]|}} [[File:CloneThrawn.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade in the [[Hand of Thrawn]]]] During the [[Caamas Document Crisis]], the Empire of the Hand was [[Mission to Nirauan|discovered]] by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] when they were searching for the source of the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] and [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|mysterious]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] that had suddenly appeared. They found the Hand of Thrawn and met Parck, Fel and Stent, still waiting for Grand Admiral Thrawn's return. Luke and Mara informed them of Thrawn's death. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] refused to join them but accepted to not reveal the existence of this shadow empire. They found an almost mature clone of Thrawn and a map of the Empire of the Hand. They decided that the clone had done nothing wrong yet so they wouldn't destroy it, but when two defense [[Droid/Legends|droids]] attacked them, they involuntarily destroyed the clone to escape. Before they did, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] managed to download an unaltered copy of the [[Caamas Document]]. Following this, [[Cavrilhu Pirates]], who were following Skywalker and Jade, attacked the Hand of Thrawn. The Hand's forces retreated, and the base was repaired and in use again by [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. In 22 ABY, the Empire of the Hand was involved in a [[Capture of the Warlord|local conflict]] on the planet [[Kariek]]. [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] of the [[501st Legion (Empire of the Hand)|501st Legion]] were dispatched to overthrow a [[The Warlord|warlord]] who had invaded and conquered Kariek, where he established a tyrannical regime. In the same year, the Empire [[Battle of the Redoubt|assisted]] the Chiss Ascendancy and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] against a third [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Third Vagaari War|invasion]]. The Vagaari were defeated in the [[The Redoubt/Legends|Chiss Redoubt]] by the Chiss, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the Empire. In the aftermath of the battle, many of the surviving colonists from [[Outbound Flight Project|Outbound Flight]] chose to join the Empire of the Hand. ===Yuuzhan Vong War=== {{Quote|There are a hundred different threats out there that would freeze your blood if you knew about them.|[[Kres'ten'tarthi|Stent]] to [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]|}} After the death of Thrawn's clone, the Empire of the Hand lost its designated heir and consequently its legitimacy. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], General Soontir Fel appeared to be the leader of the Hand. Admiral Parck's fate is unknown. Fel sent [[Force Spike]], a division of the [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] Mitth'raw'nuruodo's [[Phalanx (Chiss)|Household Phalanx]], to help the Imperial Remnant and New Republic. This force was commanded by his son, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jagged Fel|]][[Jagged Fel]]. This group would stay in the known galaxy fighting the Vong for several years. ===Disappearance=== The Empire of the Hand seemed to disappear sometime during the late Yuuzhan Vong War. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] believed that the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] destroyed it, while the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] claimed that it was merely absorbed either by the Imperial Remnant or the Chiss Ascendancy itself. However, remarks made by [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]] shortly before the [[Swarm War]] suggested that the Empire of the Hand was simply disbanded. That being said, Formbi was affiliated with the Hand, and therefore may have known that it had survived. Many of the Hand's officers, such as the Fels, joined the Ascendancy. ===Return=== {{Quote|You're being granted an honor, Admiral. You're the first to behold a secret finally come to light. You're looking at the vanguard of the Empire of the Hand.|Jagged Fel, to Natasi Daala|''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''}} Despite their absence in the years following the Yuuzhan Vong War, the Empire of the Hand had ultimately survived, far from the notice of the galaxy at large. Although rumors persisted that the Hand had either collapsed or been absorbed by the Chiss Ascendancy, [[Jagged Fel]] discovered that the Hand was still an active state. It is unknown who was in command of the Hand at this point. As [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]], Fel kept the secret of the Hand's survival to himself for security purposes. [[Natasi Daala]], the [[Plan Delta|deposed]] [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] of the [[Maw Irregular Fleet]] developed a plot to seize control of the Remnant. With his position threatened by Daala's ambition to become [[Empress/Legends|Empress]], Fel mobilized a fleet of loyal supporters and moved to destroy Daala and her subversives with a preemptive strike at [[Exodo II]]. At the same time, he requested aid from the Empire of the Hand, which dispatched a vanguard of three [[Chiss Star Destroyer]]s to aid the Remnant and its leader. After converging on Daala's position, a [[Battle over Exodo II|battle]] commenced to determine the future of the Imperial Remnant. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|Are you saying that Thrawn and Palpatine had the whole thing planned out from the beginning?''"
"''Of course they did. Just look at all the territory he opened up. He couldn't possibly have done that by himself, with just a single Star Destroyer. Palpatine must have been feeding him men and ships all along the way.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]]|}} Many of its political and social values gradually infiltrated the [[Imperial Remnant]], thanks in part to the leadership of [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], a former follower of Thrawn, as well as Jagged Fel, a warrior of the Hand who went on to be Emperor of the Remnant.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' The recruitment of aliens and female Humans into the Imperial Military's ranks, as well as the decrease in xenophobic behavior, continued as the Remnant steadily reemerged as a [[Fel Empire|resurgent Empire]] by [[130 ABY]].[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] ==Organization== ===Government=== {{Quote|And you accept nonhumans into your ranks?''"
"''As long as your world is a member of the Empire of the Hand, you're eligible.|[[Su-mil]] and [[Jorm Whistler Mackenni|Twister]]|''[[Fool's Bargain]]''}} [[File:Thrawn-SWM.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Thrawn, founder and first leader of the Empire of the Hand, a successor state to the Galactic Empire]] The Empire of the Hand composed of native [[Species/Legends|species]] that [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] found in the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] that adopted the order and efficiency of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], but without the Emperor's megalomania, the [[Tarkin Doctrine/Legends|Tarkin Doctrine]], and the institutionalized [[Speciesism|xenophobia]]. Its capital was located in the [[Hand of Thrawn]], the Grand Admiral's main fortress based on [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]].''[[Vision of the Future]]'' The Empire of the Hand operated like a [[Confederacy (disambiguation)|confederacy]] and allowed its [[Species/Legends|natives]] subjects self-rule in Wild Space as opposed to conquering them through brute force, while it worked to form alliances with the native governments of the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and protect them from the various threats that resided both within and beyond. Unlike the Galactic Empire, it accepted the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] races into the ranks of their military. The connection between the Old Empire and the Hand was kept mainly for historical aspects, but also for the psychological value as well.''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' [[File:Headofstatefel.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jagged Fel, the steward of the Empire of the Hand]] By [[44 ABY]], [[Jagged Fel]], the [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|leader]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]], also led the Empire of the Hand, however, the two roles were separate. While Fel resided on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] rather than Nirauan, he retained the power to summon [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] and other assets of the Hand.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]'' ===Military=== {{Main|Imperial Military (Empire of the Hand)}} {{Quote|Actually, I'm in the fleet end of the Imperial military. My usual command is a fleet-arm of clawcraft. And my father is very proud of me.|Chak Fel|''[[Survivor's Quest]]''}} [[File:NssisClassClawcraft-NEGVV.png|thumb|left|200px|A ''Nssis''-class Clawcraft]] The Empire of the Hand's [[Imperial Military (Empire of the Hand)|Imperial Military]] ethics were based on Thrawn's vision of war. It was an original combination of Chiss and Imperial tactics personally arranged by the Grand Admiral, such as respect of the enemies, careful consideration of [[Infantry/Legends|soldiers']] lives, surgical strikes, demonstrative invasions, preventive strikes and the significant use of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|intelligence gathering]]. [[File:Daric_Allegiance.jpg|thumb|220x220px|The Hand of Judgement, an elite squad of five Stormtrooper deserters.]] At some point, Thrawn became a [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] within Chiss society. With his new-found title, he created his [[Syndic Mitth'raw'nuruodo's Household Phalanx|own]] [[Phalanx (Chiss)|House Phalanx]], a typical Chiss paramilitary organization. Although the creation of a personal warrior retinue did not make Thrawn more respectable amongst his own people, it enabled him to recruit many Chiss sympathizers who could publicly voice their support for him. The paramilitary organization also had Phalanx soldiers, and commandos which were the elite among the elite.{{GamerCite|7|Red Sky, Blue Flame}} The Hand held [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]],''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'' [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]]''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' and many more. [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]], [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], and [[TIE/sr starfighter]]s were used as well. [[Zeta-class long-range shuttle|''Zeta''-class long-range shuttles]] and [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttles]] served as primary [[Transport/Legends|transports]] for the Hand's forces. [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]], and [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruisers]] were used as well. [[Golan space defense platform]]s also made its use with the Hand. [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]], [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]], [[Juggernaut|Imperial Juggernauts]], [[Swoop/Legends|Swoops]],{{StoryCite|story=Crisis of Faith|book=Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition}} and [[Imperial Troop Carrier]]s were used by the Hand for support of its ground forces. ===Outside relations=== The Empire of the Hand was a [[Politics|government]] that was mostly completely isolated from the wider galaxy, however it did remain in contact with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. ===Science=== With the combined technologies of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]], the Hand was able to develop new fighters. These would be called [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|''Nssis''-class Clawcraft]]. The [[maser]], a strong and powerful kinetic energy was developed as well. [[A-rack|A-Racks]] were also developed, they served as trooper carriers. They carried up to a total of ten troopers. When [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] discovered the [[Spaarti cloning cylinder|cloning cylinders]] on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], he sent many of them to the Hand. On a [[frozen moon]] within the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], Thrawn authorized a project before his [[Death/Legends|death]], which involved making [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] like warriors using deceased DNA of Jedi and Sith. ==Astrography== {{Quote|There probably aren't more than a few actual colonies out there. But you can bet there are Imperial garrisons scattered all over the place, plus intel centers and listening posts and probably a few full-blown shipyards. And if I know Thrawn, probably a whole network of alliances with the natives, too.|[[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} [[File:ThrawnsCampaigns-TEA.jpg|thumb|227px|The Empire of the Hand during the Thrawn campaign|left]] The Empire of the Hand was located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], with its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] being [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]]. It was speculated that there were few Imperial colonies in the Empire of the Hand, but it is likely that a whole network of alliances with the natives existed and the local species would colonize neighbouring planets. In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], the Wild Space [[Star system/Legends|system]] [[Kariek system/Legends|Kariek]] joined the Empire of the Hand. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= *''[[Choices of One]]'' *[[Specter of the Past (abridged audiobook)|''Specter of the Past'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Vision of the Future audiobook)}} {{1stID|Empire of the Hand|in book}} *[[Survivor's Quest (abridged audiobook)|''Survivor's Quest'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]'' *[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' *[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Balance Point'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King (abridged audiobook)|''Dark Nest I: The Joiner King'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} *''[[Crosscurrent]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Backlash]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi: Backlash (audiobook)}} *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Allies]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi:
